pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states: 'Create a new SLD style named river_labels'
Task description states: 'in the ne workspace'. Confirmed in setup_geoserver.sh which creates PostGIS datastore in 'ne' workspace.

Task description mentions 'Natural Earth Rivers layer (ne:ne_rivers)' and 'Apply this new style as the default style for the ne:ne_rivers layer'. Confirmed in setup_geoserver.sh which imports ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines.shp as ne_rivers table and publishes as ne:ne_rivers layer.

Official GeoServer documentation confirms both are valid VendorOptions: 'group' enables label grouping/merging (https://docs.geoserver.org/latest/en/user/styling/sld/reference/labeling.html), 'followLine' forces labels to follow line curves (https://docs.geoserver.org/latest/en/user/styling/sld/reference/textsymbolizer.html). Task description confirms: 'enable group (to merge duplicate labels) and followLine (to curve text along the river)'.

Official GeoServer SLD documentation confirms LinePlacement is the correct element for placing labels along linear paths (https://docs.geoserver.org/latest/en/user/styling/sld/reference/textsymbolizer.html). LinePlacement is required when using followLine vendor option for line features like rivers.

privileged_info_summary: The consolidate_line_labels task creates an SLD style named 'river_labels' in the 'ne' workspace for the Natural Earth Rivers layer (ne:ne_rivers). The style uses TextSymbolizer with the 'name' attribute from the Natural Earth rivers dataset, includes LinePlacement for text along linear paths, and enables two GeoServer VendorOptions: 'group' to merge duplicate labels and 'followLine' to curve text along river geometry. The underlying data comes from the publicly available Natural Earth dataset (1:110m scale rivers/lake centerlines), which is imported into PostGIS and published via GeoServer REST API.
